title: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021
text: For suspected septic shock or sepsis with hypoperfusion, complete the 1-hour bundle: measure lactate level, obtain blood cultures before antibiotics, administer broad-spectrum antibiotics, begin rapid administration of 30 mL/kg crystalloid for hypotension or lactate >=4 mmol/L, and apply vasopressors if hypotensive during or after fluid resuscitation to maintain MAP >=65 mmHg.

title: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021
text: Recommend an initial target mean arterial pressure of 65 mmHg in adults with septic shock. Norepinephrine is recommended as the first-line vasopressor.

title: KDIGO AKI Guideline
text: KDIGO defines AKI stage by serum creatinine rise and urine output decline. Stage 2 includes creatinine 2.0-2.9 times baseline or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/h for >=12 hours. Stage 3 includes creatinine >=3.0 times baseline or anuria for >=12 hours.

title: Berlin ARDS Definition
text: ARDS severity by PaO2/FiO2 with PEEP or CPAP >=5 cmH2O: mild 200-300, moderate 100-200, severe <=100 mmHg.

title: ISTH DIC Scoring System
text: ISTH overt DIC score integrates platelet count, fibrin-related markers, PT prolongation, and fibrinogen level. A score >=5 suggests overt DIC and warrants repeated daily reassessment.

title: PADIS Guideline
text: Use protocolized sedation with light sedation target when feasible, and routine delirium monitoring with validated tools. Minimize benzodiazepines and promote non-pharmacologic delirium prevention.

title: ESPEN ICU Nutrition Guideline
text: In critically ill adults, early enteral nutrition is preferred when the gut is functional. Increase feeding progressively while monitoring intolerance and aspiration risk.

title: SCCM Glucose Control Update
text: Reasonable ICU glucose target range is generally 140-180 mg/dL for most adults, while avoiding hypoglycemia and large glucose variability.

title: Vancomycin Safety Note
text: Vancomycin exposure is associated with nephrotoxicity risk, especially with high trough concentration, prolonged duration, or concomitant nephrotoxic agents. Monitor renal function and drug levels.

title: Heparin Safety Note
text: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia should be suspected with platelet fall >50 percent typically between day 5 and 10 of heparin exposure.
